fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer pharmacist license to missouri, pharmacist compact missouri, missouri pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Missouri Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 3605 Missouri Boulevard, P.O. Box 625, Jefferson City, MO 65102

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Missouri.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account on the Missouri Division of Professional Registration (DPR) Online Application System at professionals.mo.gov/Applications. Log in, select 'Apply for a New License', then choose 'Pharmacist' and 'Reciprocity' to complete the initial application.
steps.description_html: Create an account on the Missouri Division of Professional Registration (DPR) Online Application System. Log in, select 'Apply for a New License', then choose 'Pharmacist' and 'Reciprocity' to complete the initial application.

steps.title: Submit NABP e-Transcript
steps.description: Request your official NABP e-Transcript to be sent directly to the Missouri Board of Pharmacy by the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP).

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided in the online application to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through the Missouri State Highway Patrol and FBI. Authorization forms and instructions are available in the application portal.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Licensure
steps.description: Provide verification of your pharmacist license(s) from all states where you are currently or have been licensed, usually via NABP's electronic verification service or by direct contact with the other state boards.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the required application fee of $85 using a credit or debit card through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration (DPR) Online Application System.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration (DPR) Online Application System. The Board will notify you if any additional information is required.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Missouri. To work in Missouri, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license by endorsement in Missouri, submit your application through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration (DPR) Online Application System.

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.portal_name: Missouri Division of Professional Registration (DPR) Online Application System
official_links.registration_steps: Visit professionals.mo.gov/Applications, click 'New User Registration' to create an account with your email and a password. Once registered, log in to start a new application.
